comments.text:
## Problem

Bedrock.DataPlane.Demux.ServerTest tests intermittently fail when run as part of the full test suite, but pass when run in isolation.

## Symptoms

```
[error] Failed to get ShardServer for shard 0: {:already_started, #PID<0.4513.0>}

** (MatchError) no match of right hand side value:
    {:error, {:already_started, #PID<0.6742.0>}}
code: {:ok, shard_server} = Server.get_shard_server(server, 0)
```

Affected tests:
- test push/3 routes transaction to correct ShardServer
- test push/3 routes to multiple ShardServers  
- test durability tracking tracks minimum durable version

## Root Cause

Test isolation issue. The tests have async: false but ShardServer processes from previous tests or concurrent test modules may still be running with conflicting names/state.

## File Location

test/bedrock/data_plane/demux/server_test.exs

## Suggested Fix

1. Ensure unique shard IDs per test to avoid conflicts
2. Add explicit cleanup in test setup/teardown
3. Consider using unique process names per test run
4. Investigate if ShardServer is using global registration that persists across tests
comments.author: Jason Allum

title: Fix flaky Demux.ServerTest - ShardServer already_started errors
